``` ### Setting the line-height Use utilities like `text-sm/6` and `text-lg/7` to set the font size and line-height of an element at the same time:

ie to you boys, I was terrified. But I pressed on, and as I made my way past the breakers a strange calm came over me. I don't know if it was divine intervention or the kinship of all living things but I tell you Jerry at that moment, I was a marine biologist.

text-sm/8

So I started to walk into the water. I won't lie to you boys, I was terrified. But I pressed on, and as I made my way past the breakers a strange calm came over me. I don't know if it was divine intervention or the kinship of all living things but I tell you Jerry at that moment, I was a marine biologist.

``` ### Using a custom value ### Responsive design ## Customizing your theme You can also provide default `line-height`, `letter-spacing`, and `font-weight` values for a font size: ```css @theme { --text-tiny: 0.625rem; --text-tiny--line-height: 1.5rem; /* [!code highlight] */ --text-tiny--letter-spacing: 0.125rem; /* [!code highlight] */ --text-tiny--font-weight: 500; /* [!code highlight] */ } ```
